name: libmodule-build-perl
title: libmodule-build-perl
summary: framework for building and installing Perl modules
description:
framework for building and installing Perl modules
Module::Build is a framework for easily building, testing and installing Perl
packages. It is meant as a next-generation replacement for ExtUtils::MakeMaker
and offers many additional features characteristic of a more modern install
system, including simple subclassing for custom build actions.
.
Because it is written in Pure Perl, it has very few external dependencies. In
particular, it allows for installation of packages on systems without 'make'
and will even work without a shell. Its only prerequisites are modules that
are included with perl 5.6.0 and it is core as of perl 5.10.

commands.name: config_data
commands.description: Query or change configuration of Perl modules

arguments.description: The "config_data" tool provides a command-line interface to the configuration of Perl modules.
arguments.raw: --help  The "config_data" tool provides a command-line interface to the configuration of Perl modules.

arguments.description: If you wish, you may cre-
arguments.raw: -end  If you wish, you may cre-

arguments.description: just use the Perl API instead.
arguments.raw: -line  just use the Perl API instead.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use config_data because framework for building and installing Perl modules.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use config_data as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `config_data --module Foo::Bar --feature bazzable` as documented upstream for config_data.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `config_data --module Foo::Bar --config magic_number` as documented upstream for config_data.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `config_data --module Foo::Bar --set_feature bazzable=1` as documented upstream for config_data.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `config_data --module Foo::Bar --set_config magic_number=42` as documented upstream for config_data.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair config_data with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `config_data --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
